content.problemStatement:
Chef has NNN glasses of water numbered 111 to NNN. The ithi^{th}ith glass contains AiA_iAi​ millilitres of water.

Also, he has an unlimited supply of buckets, each having a capacity of MMM millilitres.

Consider the sequence of glasses [B1,B2,…,Bk][B_1, B_2, \dots, B_k][B1​,B2​,…,Bk​] (BjB_jBj​ denotes the water volume in the jthj^{th}jth glass) satisfying 1≤Bj≤M1 \leq B_j \leq M1≤Bj​≤M for each jjj.

We define the bucket-cost of this sequence as the number of buckets Chef uses during the following process:


Chef **takes a new empty bucket**.
If all the glasses in the sequence are empty, Chef goes to step 666.
Chef chooses the non-empty glass with the smallest number jjj (1≤j≤k1 \leq j \leq k1≤j≤k).
If there is enough place for BjB_jBj​ more millilitres of water in the bucket
Chef is holding, he pours all water from the jthj^{th}jth glass to the bucket and goes to step 222.
Chef puts aside the bucket he is holding, **takes a new empty bucket** and goes to step 222.
End.
He wants to answer QQQ queries of two types:


111 lll rrr : Calculate the bucket-cost of the sequence [Al,Al+1,…,Ar][A_l, A_{l+1}, \dots, A_r][Al​,Al+1​,…,Ar​].
222 ppp XXX : Set Ap=XA_p = XAp​=X.
In the queries of the first type Chef does not empty the glasses but only calculates the bucket-cost, i.e. queries of first type do not affect any other ones.

[Note that there is no time limit multiplier for Java in this problem]


Input Format

The first line of input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of glasses and the capacity of the buckets in milliliters, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers AiA_iAi​ — the volumes of water in milliliters in the glasses.
The third line contains a single integer QQQ — the number of queries.
The next QQQ lines describe queries in the above given format.


Output Format
For each query of the first type, output on a new line, a single integer denoting the bucket-cost of the sequence of glasses mentioned in query.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Let's count the bucket-cost of the sequence [4,10,40,4,4,7,36][4, 10, 40, 4, 4, 7, 36][4,10,40,4,4,7,36] when M=47M=47M=47.

Chef takes an empty bucket.
Pours water from the first glass into the bucket. Now the bucket contains 444 ml of water.
Pours water from the second glass into the bucket. The bucket contains 141414 ml of water.
The water from the third glass doesn't fit into the bucket. Chef takes the second bucket and pours water from the third glass. Now the bucket contains 404040 ml of water.
Chef pours the water from the fourth glass into the bucket, now it contains 444444 ml of water.
The water from the fifth glass doesn't fit into the bucket. Chef takes the third bucket and pours water from the fifth glass. Now the bucket contains 444 ml of water.
Chef pours water from the sixth glass into the bucket, now it contains 111111 ml.
Chef pours the water from the seventh glass into the bucket, now it contains 474747 ml.

Chef has used three buckets, so the bucket-cost is 333.
